2264 *
2265 * The OID of the new constraint is returned.
2266 */
2267static Oid
2268StoreRelNotNull(Relation rel, const char *nnname, AttrNumber attnum,
2269				bool is_validated, bool is_local, int inhcount,
2270				bool is_no_inherit)
2271{
2272	Oid			constrOid;
2273
2274	Assert(attnum > InvalidAttrNumber);
2275
2276	constrOid =
2277		CreateConstraintEntry(nnname,
2278							  RelationGetNamespace(rel),
2279							  CONSTRAINT_NOTNULL,
2280							  false,
2281							  false,
2282							  true, /* Is Enforced */
2283							  is_validated,
2284							  InvalidOid,
2285							  RelationGetRelid(rel),
2286							  &attnum,
2287							  1,
2288							  1,
2289							  InvalidOid,	/* not a domain constraint */
2290							  InvalidOid,	/* no associated index */
2291							  InvalidOid,	/* Foreign key fields */
2292							  NULL,
2293							  NULL,
2294							  NULL,
2295							  NULL,
2296							  0,
2297							  ' ',
2298							  ' ',
2299							  NULL,
2300							  0,
2301							  ' ',
2302							  NULL, /* not an exclusion constraint */
2303							  NULL,
2304							  NULL,
2305							  is_local,
2306							  inhcount,
2307							  is_no_inherit,
2308							  false,
2309							  false);
2310	return constrOid;
2311}
2312
2313/*
2314 * Store defaults and CHECK constraints (passed as a list of CookedConstraint).
2315 *
2316 * Each CookedConstraint struct is modified to store the new catalog tuple OID.
2317 *
2318 * NOTE: only pre-cooked expressions will be passed this way, which is to
2319 * say expressions inherited from an existing relation.  Newly parsed
2320 * expressions can be added later, by direct calls to StoreAttrDefault
2321 * and StoreRelCheck (see AddRelationNewConstraints()).
2322 */
2323static void
2324StoreConstraints(Relation rel, List *cooked_constraints, bool is_internal)
2325{
2326	int			numchecks = 0;
2327	ListCell   *lc;
2328
2329	if (cooked_constraints == NIL)
2330		return;					/* nothing to do */
2331
2332	/*
2333	 * Deparsing of constraint expressions will fail unless the just-created
2334	 * pg_attribute tuples for this relation are made visible.  So, bump the
2335	 * command counter.  CAUTION: this will cause a relcache entry rebuild.
2336	 */
2337	CommandCounterIncrement();
2338
2339	foreach(lc, cooked_constraints)
2340	{
2341		CookedConstraint *con = (CookedConstraint *) lfirst(lc);
2342
2343		switch (con->contype)
2344		{
2345			case CONSTR_DEFAULT:
2346				con->conoid = StoreAttrDefault(rel, con->attnum, con->expr,
2347											   is_internal);
2348				break;
2349			case CONSTR_CHECK:
2350				con->conoid =
2351					StoreRelCheck(rel, con->name, con->expr,
2352								  con->is_enforced, !con->skip_validation,
2353								  con->is_local, con->inhcount,
2354								  con->is_no_inherit, is_internal);
2355				numchecks++;
2356				break;
2357
2358			default:
2359				elog(ERROR, "unrecognized constraint type: %d",
2360					 (int) con->contype);
2361		}
2362	}
2363
2364	if (numchecks > 0)
2365		SetRelationNumChecks(rel, numchecks);
2366}
2367
2368/*
2369 * AddRelationNewConstraints
2370 *
2371 * Add new column default expressions and/or constraint check expressions
2372 * to an existing relation.  This is defined to do both for efficiency in
2373 * DefineRelation, but of course you can do just one or the other by passing
2374 * empty lists.
2375 *
2376 * rel: relation to be modified
2377 * newColDefaults: list of RawColumnDefault structures
2378 * newConstraints: list of Constraint nodes
2379 * allow_merge: true if check constraints may be merged with existing ones
2380 * is_local: true if definition is local, false if it's inherited
2381 * is_internal: true if result of some internal process, not a user request
2382 * queryString: used during expression transformation of default values and
2383 *		cooked CHECK constraints
2384 *
2385 * All entries in newColDefaults will be processed.  Entries in newConstraints
2386 * will be processed only if they are CONSTR_CHECK or CONSTR_NOTNULL types.
2387 *
2388 * Returns a list of CookedConstraint nodes that shows the cooked form of
2389 * the default and constraint expressions added to the relation.
2390 *
2391 * NB: caller should have opened rel with some self-conflicting lock mode,
2392 * and should hold that lock till end of transaction; for normal cases that'll
2393 * be AccessExclusiveLock, but if caller knows that the constraint is already
2394 * enforced by some other means, it can be ShareUpdateExclusiveLock.  Also, we
2395 * assume the caller has done a CommandCounterIncrement if necessary to make
2396 * the relation's catalog tuples visible.
2397 */
2398List *
2399AddRelationNewConstraints(Relation rel,
2400						  List *newColDefaults,
2401						  List *newConstraints,
2402						  bool allow_merge,
2403						  bool is_local,
2404						  bool is_internal,
2405						  const char *queryString)
2406{
2407	List	   *cookedConstraints = NIL;
2408	TupleDesc	tupleDesc;
2409	TupleConstr *oldconstr;
2410	int			numoldchecks;
2411	ParseState *pstate;
2412	ParseNamespaceItem *nsitem;
2413	int			numchecks;
2414	List	   *checknames;
2415	List	   *nnnames;
2416	Node	   *expr;
2417	CookedConstraint *cooked;
2418
2419	/*
2420	 * Get info about existing constraints.
2421	 */
2422	tupleDesc = RelationGetDescr(rel);
2423	oldconstr = tupleDesc->constr;
2424	if (oldconstr)
2425		numoldchecks = oldconstr->num_check;
2426	else
2427		numoldchecks = 0;
2428
2429	/*
2430	 * Create a dummy ParseState and insert the target relation as its sole
2431	 * rangetable entry.  We need a ParseState for transformExpr.
2432	 */
2433	pstate = make_parsestate(NULL);
2434	pstate->p_sourcetext = queryString;
2435	nsitem = addRangeTableEntryForRelation(pstate,
2436										   rel,
2437										   AccessShareLock,
2438										   NULL,
2439										   false,
2440										   true);
2441	addNSItemToQuery(pstate, nsitem, true, true, true);
2442
2443	/*
2444	 * Process column default expressions.
2445	 */
2446	foreach_ptr(RawColumnDefault, colDef, newColDefaults)
2447	{
2448		Form_pg_attribute atp = TupleDescAttr(rel->rd_att, colDef->attnum - 1);
2449		Oid			defOid;
2450
2451		expr = cookDefault(pstate, colDef->raw_default,
2452						   atp->atttypid, atp->atttypmod,
2453						   NameStr(atp->attname),
2454						   atp->attgenerated);
2455
2456		/*
2457		 * If the expression is just a NULL constant, we do not bother to make
2458		 * an explicit pg_attrdef entry, since the default behavior is
2459		 * equivalent.  This applies to column defaults, but not for
2460		 * generation expressions.
2461		 *
2462		 * Note a nonobvious property of this test: if the column is of a
2463		 * domain type, what we'll get is not a bare null Const but a
2464		 * CoerceToDomain expr, so we will not discard the default.  This is
2465		 * critical because the column default needs to be retained to
2466		 * override any default that the domain might have.
2467		 */
2468		if (expr == NULL ||
2469			(!colDef->generated &&
2470			 IsA(expr, Const) &&
2471			 castNode(Const, expr)->constisnull))
2472			continue;
2473
2474		defOid = StoreAttrDefault(rel, colDef->attnum, expr, is_internal);
2475
2476		cooked = (CookedConstraint *) palloc(sizeof(CookedConstraint));
2477		cooked->contype = CONSTR_DEFAULT;
2478		cooked->conoid = defOid;
2479		cooked->name = NULL;
2480		cooked->attnum = colDef->attnum;
2481		cooked->expr = expr;
2482		cooked->is_enforced = true;
2483		cooked->skip_validation = false;
2484		cooked->is_local = is_local;
2485		cooked->inhcount = is_local ? 0 : 1;
2486		cooked->is_no_inherit = false;
2487		cookedConstraints = lappend(cookedConstraints, cooked);
2488	}
2489
2490	/*
2491	 * Process constraint expressions.
2492	 */
2493	numchecks = numoldchecks;
2494	checknames = NIL;
2495	nnnames = NIL;
2496	foreach_node(Constraint, cdef, newConstraints)
2497	{
2498		Oid			constrOid;
2499
2500		if (cdef->contype == CONSTR_CHECK)
2501		{
2502			char	   *ccname;
2503
2504			if (cdef->raw_expr != NULL)
2505			{
2506				Assert(cdef->cooked_expr == NULL);
2507
2508				/*
2509				 * Transform raw parsetree to executable expression, and
2510				 * verify it's valid as a CHECK constraint.
2511				 */
2512				expr = cookConstraint(pstate, cdef->raw_expr,
2513									  RelationGetRelationName(rel));
2514			}
2515			else
2516			{
2517				Assert(cdef->cooked_expr != NULL);
2518
2519				/*
2520				 * Here, we assume the parser will only pass us valid CHECK
2521				 * expressions, so we do no particular checking.
2522				 */
2523				expr = stringToNode(cdef->cooked_expr);
2524			}
2525
2526			/*
2527			 * Check name uniqueness, or generate a name if none was given.
2528			 */
2529			if (cdef->conname != NULL)
2530			{
2531				ccname = cdef->conname;
2532				/* Check against other new constraints */
2533				/* Needed because we don't do CommandCounterIncrement in loop */
2534				foreach_ptr(char, chkname, checknames)
2535				{
2536					if (strcmp(chkname, ccname) == 0)
2537						ereport(ERROR,
2538								(errcode(ERRCODE_DUPLICATE_OBJECT),
2539								 errmsg("check constraint \"%s\" already exists",
2540										ccname)));
2541				}
2542
2543				/* save name for future checks */
2544				checknames = lappend(checknames, ccname);
2545
2546				/*
2547				 * Check against pre-existing constraints.  If we are allowed
2548				 * to merge with an existing constraint, there's no more to do
2549				 * here. (We omit the duplicate constraint from the result,
2550				 * which is what ATAddCheckNNConstraint wants.)
2551				 */
2552				if (MergeWithExistingConstraint(rel, ccname, expr,
2553												allow_merge, is_local,
2554												cdef->is_enforced,
2555												cdef->initially_valid,
2556												cdef->is_no_inherit))
2557					continue;
2558			}
2559			else
2560			{
2561				/*
2562				 * When generating a name, we want to create "tab_col_check"
2563				 * for a column constraint and "tab_check" for a table
2564				 * constraint.  We no longer have any info about the syntactic
2565				 * positioning of the constraint phrase, so we approximate
2566				 * this by seeing whether the expression references more than
2567				 * one column.  (If the user played by the rules, the result
2568				 * is the same...)
2569				 *
2570				 * Note: pull_var_clause() doesn't descend into sublinks, but
2571				 * we eliminated those above; and anyway this only needs to be
2572				 * an approximate answer.
2573				 */
2574				List	   *vars;
2575				char	   *colname;
2576
2577				vars = pull_var_clause(expr, 0);
2578
2579				/* eliminate duplicates */
2580				vars = list_union(NIL, vars);
2581
2582				if (list_length(vars) == 1)
2583					colname = get_attname(RelationGetRelid(rel),
2584										  ((Var *) linitial(vars))->varattno,
2585										  true);
2586				else
2587					colname = NULL;
2588
2589				ccname = ChooseConstraintName(RelationGetRelationName(rel),
2590											  colname,
2591											  "check",
2592											  RelationGetNamespace(rel),
2593											  checknames);
2594
2595				/* save name for future checks */
2596				checknames = lappend(checknames, ccname);
2597			}
2598
2599			/*
2600			 * OK, store it.
2601			 */
2602			constrOid =
2603				StoreRelCheck(rel, ccname, expr, cdef->is_enforced,
2604							  cdef->initially_valid, is_local,
2605							  is_local ? 0 : 1, cdef->is_no_inherit,
2606							  is_internal);
2607
2608			numchecks++;
2609
2610			cooked = (CookedConstraint *) palloc(sizeof(CookedConstraint));
2611			cooked->contype = CONSTR_CHECK;
2612			cooked->conoid = constrOid;
2613			cooked->name = ccname;
2614			cooked->attnum = 0;
2615			cooked->expr = expr;
2616			cooked->is_enforced = cdef->is_enforced;
2617			cooked->skip_validation = cdef->skip_validation;
2618			cooked->is_local = is_local;
2619			cooked->inhcount = is_local ? 0 : 1;
2620			cooked->is_no_inherit = cdef->is_no_inherit;
2621			cookedConstraints = lappend(cookedConstraints, cooked);
2622		}
2623		else if (cdef->contype == CONSTR_NOTNULL)
2624		{
2625			CookedConstraint *nncooked;
2626			AttrNumber	colnum;
2627			int16		inhcount = is_local ? 0 : 1;
2628			char	   *nnname;
2629
2630			/* Determine which column to modify */
2631			colnum = get_attnum(RelationGetRelid(rel), strVal(linitial(cdef->keys)));
2632			if (colnum == InvalidAttrNumber)
2633				ereport(ERROR,
2634						errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_COLUMN),
2635						errmsg("column \"%s\" of relation \"%s\" does not exist",
2636							   strVal(linitial(cdef->keys)), RelationGetRelationName(rel)));
2637			if (colnum < InvalidAttrNumber)
2638				ereport(ERROR,
2639						errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
2640						errmsg("cannot add not-null constraint on system column \"%s\"",
2641							   strVal(linitial(cdef->keys))));
2642
2643			Assert(cdef->initially_valid != cdef->skip_validation);
2644
2645			/*
2646			 * If the column already has a not-null constraint, we don't want
2647			 * to add another one; adjust inheritance status as needed.  This
2648			 * also checks whether the existing constraint matches the
2649			 * requested validity.
2650			 */
2651			if (AdjustNotNullInheritance(RelationGetRelid(rel), colnum,
2652										 cdef->conname,
2653										 is_local, cdef->is_no_inherit,
2654										 cdef->skip_validation))
2655				continue;
2656
2657			/*
2658			 * If a constraint name is specified, check that it isn't already
2659			 * used.  Otherwise, choose a non-conflicting one ourselves.
2660			 */
2661			if (cdef->conname)
2662			{
2663				if (ConstraintNameIsUsed(CONSTRAINT_RELATION,
2664										 RelationGetRelid(rel),
2665										 cdef->conname))
2666					ereport(ERROR,
2667							errcode(ERRCODE_DUPLICATE_OBJECT),
2668							errmsg("constraint \"%s\" for relation \"%s\" already exists",
2669								   cdef->conname, RelationGetRelationName(rel)));
2670				nnname = cdef->conname;
2671			}
2672			else
2673				nnname = ChooseConstraintName(RelationGetRelationName(rel),
2674											  strVal(linitial(cdef->keys)),
2675											  "not_null",
2676											  RelationGetNamespace(rel),
2677											  nnnames);
2678			nnnames = lappend(nnnames, nnname);
2679
2680			constrOid =
2681				StoreRelNotNull(rel, nnname, colnum,
2682								cdef->initially_valid,
2683								is_local,
2684								inhcount,
2685								cdef->is_no_inherit);
2686
2687			nncooked = (CookedConstraint *) palloc(sizeof(CookedConstraint));
2688			nncooked->contype = CONSTR_NOTNULL;
2689			nncooked->conoid = constrOid;
2690			nncooked->name = nnname;
2691			nncooked->attnum = colnum;
2692			nncooked->expr = NULL;
2693			nncooked->is_enforced = true;
2694			nncooked->skip_validation = cdef->skip_validation;
2695			nncooked->is_local = is_local;
2696			nncooked->inhcount = inhcount;
2697			nncooked->is_no_inherit = cdef->is_no_inherit;
2698
2699			cookedConstraints = lappend(cookedConstraints, nncooked);
2700		}
2701	}
2702
2703	/*
2704	 * Update the count of constraints in the relation's pg_class tuple. We do
2705	 * this even if there was no change, in order to ensure that an SI update
2706	 * message is sent out for the pg_class tuple, which will force other
2707	 * backends to rebuild their relcache entries for the rel. (This is
2708	 * critical if we added defaults but not constraints.)
2709	 */
2710	SetRelationNumChecks(rel, numchecks);
2711
2712	return cookedConstraints;
2713}
2714
2715/*
2716 * Check for a pre-existing check constraint that conflicts with a proposed
2717 * new one, and either adjust its conislocal/coninhcount settings or throw
2718 * error as needed.
2719 *
2720 * Returns true if merged (constraint is a duplicate), or false if it's
2721 * got a so-far-unique name, or throws error if conflict.
2722 *
2723 * XXX See MergeConstraintsIntoExisting too if you change this code.
2724 */
2725static bool
2726MergeWithExistingConstraint(Relation rel, const char *ccname, Node *expr,
2727							bool allow_merge, bool is_local,
2728							bool is_enforced,
2729							bool is_initially_valid,
2730							bool is_no_inherit)
2731{
2732	bool		found;
2733	Relation	conDesc;
2734	SysScanDesc conscan;
2735	ScanKeyData skey[3];
2736	HeapTuple	tup;
2737
2738	/* Search for a pg_constraint entry with same name and relation */
2739	conDesc = table_open(ConstraintRelationId, RowExclusiveLock);
2740
2741	found = false;
2742
2743	ScanKeyInit(&skey[0],
2744				Anum_pg_constraint_conrelid,
2745				BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_OIDEQ,
2746				ObjectIdGetDatum(RelationGetRelid(rel)));
2747	ScanKeyInit(&skey[1],
2748				Anum_pg_constraint_contypid,
2749				BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_OIDEQ,
2750				ObjectIdGetDatum(InvalidOid));
2751	ScanKeyInit(&skey[2],
2752				Anum_pg_constraint_conname,
2753				BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_NAMEEQ,
2754				CStringGetDatum(ccname));
2755
2756	conscan = systable_beginscan(conDesc, ConstraintRelidTypidNameIndexId, true,
2757								 NULL, 3, skey);
2758
2759	/* There can be at most one matching row */
2760	if (HeapTupleIsValid(tup = systable_getnext(conscan)))
2761	{
2762		Form_pg_constraint con = (Form_pg_constraint) GETSTRUCT(tup);
2763
2764		/* Found it.  Conflicts if not identical check constraint */
2765		if (con->contype == CONSTRAINT_CHECK)
2766		{
2767			Datum		val;
2768			bool		isnull;
2769
2770			val = fastgetattr(tup,
2771							  Anum_pg_constraint_conbin,
2772							  conDesc->rd_att, &isnull);
2773			if (isnull)
2774				elog(ERROR, "null conbin for rel %s",
2775					 RelationGetRelationName(rel));
2776			if (equal(expr, stringToNode(TextDatumGetCString(val))))
2777				found = true;
2778		}
2779
2780		/*
2781		 * If the existing constraint is purely inherited (no local
2782		 * definition) then interpret addition of a local constraint as a
2783		 * legal merge.  This allows ALTER ADD CONSTRAINT on parent and child
2784		 * tables to be given in either order with same end state.  However if
2785		 * the relation is a partition, all inherited constraints are always
2786		 * non-local, including those that were merged.
2787		 */
2788		if (is_local && !con->conislocal && !rel->rd_rel->relispartition)
2789			allow_merge = true;
2790
2791		if (!found || !allow_merge)
2792			ereport(ERROR,
2793					(errcode(ERRCODE_DUPLICATE_OBJECT),
2794					 errmsg("constraint \"%s\" for relation \"%s\" already exists",
2795							ccname, RelationGetRelationName(rel))));
2796
2797		/* If the child constraint is "no inherit" then cannot merge */
2798		if (con->connoinherit)
2799			ereport(ERROR,
2800					(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_OBJECT_DEFINITION),
2801					 errmsg("constraint \"%s\" conflicts with non-inherited constraint on relation \"%s\"",
2802							ccname, RelationGetRelationName(rel))));
2803
2804		/*
2805		 * Must not change an existing inherited constraint to "no inherit"
2806		 * status.  That's because inherited constraints should be able to
2807		 * propagate to lower-level children.
2808		 */
2809		if (con->coninhcount > 0 && is_no_inherit)
2810			ereport(ERROR,
2811					(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_OBJECT_DEFINITION),
2812					 errmsg("constraint \"%s\" conflicts with inherited constraint on relation \"%s\"",
2813							ccname, RelationGetRelationName(rel))));
2814
2815		/*
2816		 * If the child constraint is "not valid" then cannot merge with a
2817		 * valid parent constraint.
2818		 */
2819		if (is_initially_valid && con->conenforced && !con->convalidated)
2820			ereport(ERROR,
2821					(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_OBJECT_DEFINITION),
2822					 errmsg("constraint \"%s\" conflicts with NOT VALID constraint on relation \"%s\"",
2823							ccname, RelationGetRelationName(rel))));
2824
2825		/*
2826		 * A non-enforced child constraint cannot be merged with an enforced
2827		 * parent constraint. However, the reverse is allowed, where the child
2828		 * constraint is enforced.
2829		 */
2830		if ((!is_local && is_enforced && !con->conenforced) ||
2831			(is_local && !is_enforced && con->conenforced))
2832			ereport(ERROR,
2833					(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_OBJECT_DEFINITION),
2834					 errmsg("constraint \"%s\" conflicts with NOT ENFORCED constraint on relation \"%s\"",
2835							ccname, RelationGetRelationName(rel))));
2836
2837		/* OK to update the tuple */
2838		ereport(NOTICE,
2839				(errmsg("merging constraint \"%s\" with inherited definition",
2840						ccname)));
2841
2842		tup = heap_copytuple(tup);
2843		con = (Form_pg_constraint) GETSTRUCT(tup);
2844
2845		/*
2846		 * In case of partitions, an inherited constraint must be inherited
2847		 * only once since it cannot have multiple parents and it is never
2848		 * considered local.
2849		 */
2850		if (rel->rd_rel->relispartition)
2851		{
2852			con->coninhcount = 1;
2853			con->conislocal = false;
2854		}
2855		else
2856		{
2857			if (is_local)
2858				con->conislocal = true;
2859			else if (pg_add_s16_overflow(con->coninhcount, 1,
2860										 &con->coninhcount))
2861				ereport(ERROR,
2862						errcode(ERRCODE_PROGRAM_LIMIT_EXCEEDED),
2863						errmsg("too many inheritance parents"));
2864		}
2865
2866		if (is_no_inherit)
2867		{
2868			Assert(is_local);
2869			con->connoinherit = true;
2870		}
2871
2872		/*
2873		 * If the child constraint is required to be enforced while the parent
2874		 * constraint is not, this should be allowed by marking the child
2875		 * constraint as enforced. In the reverse case, an error would have
2876		 * already been thrown before reaching this point.
2877		 */
2878		if (is_enforced && !con->conenforced)
2879		{
2880			Assert(is_local);
2881			con->conenforced = true;
2882			con->convalidated = true;
2883		}
2884
2885		CatalogTupleUpdate(conDesc, &tup->t_self, tup);
2886	}
2887
2888	systable_endscan(conscan);
2889	table_close(conDesc, RowExclusiveLock);
2890
2891	return found;
2892}
2893
2894/*
2895 * Create the not-null constraints when creating a new relation
2896 *
2897 * These come from two sources: the 'constraints' list (of Constraint) is
2898 * specified directly by the user; the 'old_notnulls' list (of
2899 * CookedConstraint) comes from inheritance.  We create one constraint
2900 * for each column, giving priority to user-specified ones, and setting
2901 * inhcount according to how many parents cause each column to get a
2902 * not-null constraint.  If a user-specified name clashes with another
2903 * user-specified name, an error is raised.  'existing_constraints'
2904 * is a list of already defined constraint names, which should be avoided
2905 * when generating further ones.
2906 *
2907 * Returns a list of AttrNumber for columns that need to have the attnotnull
2908 * flag set.
2909 */
2910List *
2911AddRelationNotNullConstraints(Relation rel, List *constraints,
2912							  List *old_notnulls, List *existing_constraints)
2913{
2914	List	   *givennames;
2915	List	   *nnnames;
2916	List	   *nncols = NIL;
2917
2918	/*
2919	 * We track two lists of names: nnnames keeps all the constraint names,
2920	 * givennames tracks user-generated names.  The distinction is important,
2921	 * because we must raise error for user-generated name conflicts, but for
2922	 * system-generated name conflicts we just generate another.
2923	 */
2924	nnnames = list_copy(existing_constraints);	/* don't scribble on input */
2925	givennames = NIL;
2926
2927	/*
2928	 * First, create all not-null constraints that are directly specified by
2929	 * the user.  Note that inheritance might have given us another source for
2930	 * each, so we must scan the old_notnulls list and increment inhcount for
2931	 * each element with identical attnum.  We delete from there any element
2932	 * that we process.
2933	 *
2934	 * We don't use foreach() here because we have two nested loops over the
2935	 * constraint list, with possible element deletions in the inner one. If
2936	 * we used foreach_delete_current() it could only fix up the state of one
2937	 * of the loops, so it seems cleaner to use looping over list indexes for
2938	 * both loops.  Note that any deletion will happen beyond where the outer
2939	 * loop is, so its index never needs adjustment.
2940	 */
2941	for (int outerpos = 0; outerpos < list_length(constraints); outerpos++)
2942	{
2943		Constraint *constr;
2944		AttrNumber	attnum;
2945		char	   *conname;
2946		int			inhcount = 0;
2947
2948		constr = list_nth_node(Constraint, constraints, outerpos);
2949
2950		Assert(constr->contype == CONSTR_NOTNULL);
2951
2952		attnum = get_attnum(RelationGetRelid(rel),
2953							strVal(linitial(constr->keys)));
2954		if (attnum == InvalidAttrNumber)
2955			ereport(ERROR,
2956					errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_COLUMN),
2957					errmsg("column \"%s\" of relation \"%s\" does not exist",
2958						   strVal(linitial(constr->keys)),
2959						   RelationGetRelationName(rel)));
2960		if (attnum < InvalidAttrNumber)
2961			ereport(ERROR,
2962					errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
2963					errmsg("cannot add not-null constraint on system column \"%s\"",
2964						   strVal(linitial(constr->keys))));
2965
2966		/*
2967		 * A column can only have one not-null constraint, so discard any
2968		 * additional ones that appear for columns we already saw; but check
2969		 * that the NO INHERIT flags match.
2970		 */
2971		for (int restpos = outerpos + 1; restpos < list_length(constraints);)
2972		{
2973			Constraint *other;
2974
2975			other = list_nth_node(Constraint, constraints, restpos);
2976			if (strcmp(strVal(linitial(constr->keys)),
2977					   strVal(linitial(other->keys))) == 0)
2978			{
2979				if (other->is_no_inherit != constr->is_no_inherit)
2980					ereport(ERROR,
2981							errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
2982							errmsg("conflicting NO INHERIT declaration for not-null constraint on column \"%s\"",
2983								   strVal(linitial(constr->keys))));
2984
2985				/*
2986				 * Preserve constraint name if one is specified, but raise an
2987				 * error if conflicting ones are specified.
2988				 */
2989				if (other->conname)
2990				{
2991					if (!constr->conname)
2992						constr->conname = pstrdup(other->conname);
2993					else if (strcmp(constr->conname, other->conname) != 0)
2994						ereport(ERROR,
2995								errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
2996								errmsg("conflicting not-null constraint names \"%s\" and \"%s\"",
2997									   constr->conname, other->conname));
2998				}
2999
3000				/* XXX do we need to verify any other fields? */
3001				constraints = list_delete_nth_cell(constraints, restpos);
3002			}
3003			else
3004				restpos++;
3005		}
3006
3007		/*
3008		 * Search in the list of inherited constraints for any entries on the
3009		 * same column; determine an inheritance count from that.  Also, if at
3010		 * least one parent has a constraint for this column, then we must not
3011		 * accept a user specification for a NO INHERIT one.  Any constraint
3012		 * from parents that we process here is deleted from the list: we no
3013		 * longer need to process it in the loop below.
3014		 */
3015		foreach_ptr(CookedConstraint, old, old_notnulls)
3016		{
3017			if (old->attnum == attnum)
3018			{
3019				/*
3020				 * If we get a constraint from the parent, having a local NO
3021				 * INHERIT one doesn't work.
3022				 */
3023				if (constr->is_no_inherit)
3024					ereport(ERROR,
3025							(errcode(ERRCODE_DATATYPE_MISMATCH),
3026							 errmsg("cannot define not-null constraint with NO INHERIT on column \"%s\"",
3027									strVal(linitial(constr->keys))),
3028							 errdetail("The column has an inherited not-null constraint.")));
3029
3030				inhcount++;
3031				old_notnulls = foreach_delete_current(old_notnulls, old);
3032			}
3033		}
3034
3035		/*
3036		 * Determine a constraint name, which may have been specified by the
3037		 * user, or raise an error if a conflict exists with another
3038		 * user-specified name.
3039		 */
3040		if (constr->conname)
3041		{
3042			foreach_ptr(char, thisname, givennames)
3043			{
3044				if (strcmp(thisname, constr->conname) == 0)
3045					ereport(ERROR,
3046							errcode(ERRCODE_DUPLICATE_OBJECT),
3047							errmsg("constraint \"%s\" for relation \"%s\" already exists",
3048								   constr->conname,
3049								   RelationGetRelationName(rel)));
3050			}
3051
3052			conname = constr->conname;
3053			givennames = lappend(givennames, conname);
3054		}
3055		else
3056			conname = ChooseConstraintName(RelationGetRelationName(rel),
3057										   get_attname(RelationGetRelid(rel),
3058													   attnum, false),
3059										   "not_null",
3060										   RelationGetNamespace(rel),
3061										   nnnames);
3062		nnnames = lappend(nnnames, conname);
3063
3064		StoreRelNotNull(rel, conname,
3065						attnum, true, true,
3066						inhcount, constr->is_no_inherit);
3067
3068		nncols = lappend_int(nncols, attnum);
3069	}
3070
3071	/*
3072	 * If any column remains in the old_notnulls list, we must create a not-
3073	 * null constraint marked not-local for that column.  Because multiple
3074	 * parents could specify a not-null constraint for the same column, we
3075	 * must count how many there are and set an appropriate inhcount
3076	 * accordingly, deleting elements we've already processed.
3077	 *
3078	 * We don't use foreach() here because we have two nested loops over the
3079	 * constraint list, with possible element deletions in the inner one. If
3080	 * we used foreach_delete_current() it could only fix up the state of one
3081	 * of the loops, so it seems cleaner to use looping over list indexes for
3082	 * both loops.  Note that any deletion will happen beyond where the outer
3083	 * loop is, so its index never needs adjustment.
3084	 */
3085	for (int outerpos = 0; outerpos < list_length(old_notnulls); outerpos++)
3086	{
3087		CookedConstraint *cooked;
3088		char	   *conname = NULL;
3089		int			inhcount = 1;
3090
3091		cooked = (CookedConstraint *) list_nth(old_notnulls, outerpos);
3092		Assert(cooked->contype == CONSTR_NOTNULL);
3093		Assert(cooked->name);
3094
3095		/*
3096		 * Preserve the first non-conflicting constraint name we come across.
3097		 */
3098		if (conname == NULL)
3099			conname = cooked->name;
3100
3101		for (int restpos = outerpos + 1; restpos < list_length(old_notnulls);)
3102		{
3103			CookedConstraint *other;
3104
3105			other = (CookedConstraint *) list_nth(old_notnulls, restpos);
3106			Assert(other->name);
3107			if (other->attnum == cooked->attnum)
3108			{
3109				if (conname == NULL)
3110					conname = other->name;
3111
3112				inhcount++;
3113				old_notnulls = list_delete_nth_cell(old_notnulls, restpos);
3114			}
3115			else
3116				restpos++;
3117		}
3118
3119		/* If we got a name, make sure it isn't one we've already used */
3120		if (conname != NULL)
3121		{
3122			foreach_ptr(char, thisname, nnnames)
3123			{
3124				if (strcmp(thisname, conname) == 0)
3125				{
3126					conname = NULL;
3127					break;
3128				}
3129			}
3130		}
3131
3132		/* and choose a name, if needed */
3133		if (conname == NULL)
3134			conname = ChooseConstraintName(RelationGetRelationName(rel),
3135										   get_attname(RelationGetRelid(rel),
3136													   cooked->attnum, false),
3137										   "not_null",
3138										   RelationGetNamespace(rel),
3139										   nnnames);
3140		nnnames = lappend(nnnames, conname);
3141
3142		/* ignore the origin constraint's is_local and inhcount */
3143		StoreRelNotNull(rel, conname, cooked->attnum, true,
3144						false, inhcount, false);
3145
3146		nncols = lappend_int(nncols, cooked->attnum);
3147	}
3148
3149	return nncols;
3150}
3151
3152/*
3153 * Update the count of constraints in the relation's pg_class tuple.
3154 *
3155 * Caller had better hold exclusive lock on the relation.
3156 *
3157 * An important side effect is that a SI update message will be sent out for
3158 * the pg_class tuple, which will force other backends to rebuild their
3159 * relcache entries for the rel.  Also, this backend will rebuild its
3160 * own relcache entry at the next CommandCounterIncrement.
3161 */
3162static void
3163SetRelationNumChecks(Relation rel, int numchecks)
3164{
3165	Relation	relrel;
3166	HeapTuple	reltup;
3167	Form_pg_class relStruct;
3168
3169	relrel = table_open(RelationRelationId, RowExclusiveLock);
3170	reltup = SearchSysCacheCopy1(RELOID,
3171								 ObjectIdGetDatum(RelationGetRelid(rel)));
3172	if (!HeapTupleIsValid(reltup))
3173		elog(ERROR, "cache lookup failed for relation %u",
3174			 RelationGetRelid(rel));
3175	relStruct = (Form_pg_class) GETSTRUCT(reltup);
3176
3177	if (relStruct->relchecks != numchecks)
3178	{
3179		relStruct->relchecks = numchecks;
3180
3181		CatalogTupleUpdate(relrel, &reltup->t_self, reltup);
3182	}
3183	else
3184	{
3185		/* Skip the disk update, but force relcache inval anyway */
3186		CacheInvalidateRelcache(rel);
3187	}
3188
3189	heap_freetuple(reltup);
3190	table_close(relrel, RowExclusiveLock);
3191}
3192
3193/*
3194 * Check for references to generated columns
3195 */
3196static bool
3197check_nested_generated_walker(Node *node, void *context)
3198{
3199	ParseState *pstate = context;
3200
3201	if (node == NULL)
3202		return false;
3203	else if (IsA(node, Var))
3204	{
3205		Var		   *var = (Var *) node;
3206		Oid			relid;
3207		AttrNumber	attnum;
3208
3209		relid = rt_fetch(var->varno, pstate->p_rtable)->relid;
3210		if (!OidIsValid(relid))
3211			return false;		/* XXX shouldn't we raise an error? */
3212
3213		attnum = var->varattno;
3214
3215		if (attnum > 0 && get_attgenerated(relid, attnum))
3216			ereport(ERROR,
3217					(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_OBJECT_DEFINITION),
3218					 errmsg("cannot use generated column \"%s\" in column generation expression",
3219							get_attname(relid, attnum, false)),
3220					 errdetail("A generated column cannot reference another generated column."),
3221					 parser_errposition(pstate, var->location)));
3222		/* A whole-row Var is necessarily self-referential, so forbid it */
3223		if (attnum == 0)
3224			ereport(ERROR,
3225					(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_OBJECT_DEFINITION),
3226					 errmsg("cannot use whole-row variable in column generation expression"),
3227					 errdetail("This would cause the generated column to depend on its own value."),
3228					 parser_errposition(pstate, var->location)));
3229		/* System columns were already checked in the parser */
3230
3231		return false;
3232	}
3233	else
3234		return expression_tree_walker(node, check_nested_generated_walker,
3235									  context);
3236}
3237
3238static void
3239check_nested_generated(ParseState *pstate, Node *node)
3240{
3241	check_nested_generated_walker(node, pstate);
3242}
3243
3244/*
3245 * Check security of virtual generated column expression.
3246 *
3247 * Just like selecting from a view is exploitable (CVE-2024-7348), selecting
3248 * from a table with virtual generated columns is exploitable.  Users who are
3249 * concerned about this can avoid selecting from views, but telling them to
3250 * avoid selecting from tables is less practical.
3251 *
3252 * To address this, this restricts generation expressions for virtual
3253 * generated columns are restricted to using built-in functions and types.  We
3254 * assume that built-in functions and types cannot be exploited for this
3255 * purpose.  Note the overall security also requires that all functions in use
3256 * a immutable.  (For example, there are some built-in non-immutable functions
3257 * that can run arbitrary SQL.)  The immutability is checked elsewhere, since
3258 * that is a property that needs to hold independent of security
3259 * considerations.
3260 *
3261 * In the future, this could be expanded by some new mechanism to declare
3262 * other functions and types as safe or trusted for this purpose, but that is
3263 * to be designed.
3264 */
3265
3266/*
3267 * Callback for check_functions_in_node() that determines whether a function
3268 * is user-defined.
3269 */
3270static bool
3271contains_user_functions_checker(Oid func_id, void *context)
3272{
3273	return (func_id >= FirstUnpinnedObjectId);
3274}
3275
3276/*
3277 * Checks for all the things we don't want in the generation expressions of
3278 * virtual generated columns for security reasons.  Errors out if it finds
3279 * one.
3280 */
3281static bool
3282check_virtual_generated_security_walker(Node *node, void *context)
3283{
3284	ParseState *pstate = context;
3285
3286	if (node == NULL)
3287		return false;
3288
3289	if (!IsA(node, List))
3290	{
3291		if (check_functions_in_node(node, contains_user_functions_checker, NULL))
3292			ereport(ERROR,
3293					errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
3294					errmsg("generation expression uses user-defined function"),
3295					errdetail("Virtual generated columns that make use of user-defined functions are not yet supported."),
3296					parser_errposition(pstate, exprLocation(node)));
3297
3298		/*
3299		 * check_functions_in_node() doesn't check some node types (see
3300		 * comment there).  We handle CoerceToDomain and MinMaxExpr by
3301		 * checking for built-in types.  The other listed node types cannot
3302		 * call user-definable SQL-visible functions.
3303		 *
3304		 * We furthermore need this type check to handle built-in, immutable
3305		 * polymorphic functions such as array_eq().
3306		 */
3307		if (exprType(node) >= FirstUnpinnedObjectId)
3308			ereport(ERROR,
3309					errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
3310					errmsg("generation expression uses user-defined type"),
3311					errdetail("Virtual generated columns that make use of user-defined types are not yet supported."),
3312					parser_errposition(pstate, exprLocation(node)));
3313	}
3314
3315	return expression_tree_walker(node, check_virtual_generated_security_walker, context);
3316}
3317
3318static void
3319check_virtual_generated_security(ParseState *pstate, Node *node)
3320{
3321	check_virtual_generated_security_walker(node, pstate);
3322}
3323
3324/*
3325 * Take a raw default and convert it to a cooked format ready for
3326 * storage.
3327 *
3328 * Parse state should be set up to recognize any vars that might appear
3329 * in the expression.  (Even though we plan to reject vars, it's more
3330 * user-friendly to give the correct error message than "unknown var".)
3331 *
3332 * If atttypid is not InvalidOid, coerce the expression to the specified
3333 * type (and typmod atttypmod).   attname is only needed in this case:
3334 * it is used in the error message, if any.
3335 */
3336Node *
3337cookDefault(ParseState *pstate,
3338			Node *raw_default,
3339			Oid atttypid,
3340			int32 atttypmod,
3341			const char *attname,
3342			char attgenerated)
3343{
3344	Node	   *expr;
3345
3346	Assert(raw_default != NULL);
3347
3348	/*
3349	 * Transform raw parsetree to executable expression.
3350	 */
3351	expr = transformExpr(pstate, raw_default, attgenerated ? EXPR_KIND_GENERATED_COLUMN : EXPR_KIND_COLUMN_DEFAULT);
3352
3353	if (attgenerated)
3354	{
3355		/* Disallow refs to other generated columns */
3356		check_nested_generated(pstate, expr);
3357
3358		/* Disallow mutable functions */
3359		if (contain_mutable_functions_after_planning((Expr *) expr))
3360			ereport(ERROR,
3361					(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_OBJECT_DEFINITION),
3362					 errmsg("generation expression is not immutable")));
3363
3364		/* Check security of expressions for virtual generated column */
3365		if (attgenerated == ATTRIBUTE_GENERATED_VIRTUAL)
3366			check_virtual_generated_security(pstate, expr);
3367	}
3368	else
3369	{
3370		/*
3371		 * For a default expression, transformExpr() should have rejected
3372		 * column references.
3373		 */
3374		Assert(!contain_var_clause(expr));
3375	}
3376
3377	/*
3378	 * Coerce the expression to the correct type and typmod, if given. This
3379	 * should match the parser's processing of non-defaulted expressions ---
3380	 * see transformAssignedExpr().
3381	 */
3382	if (OidIsValid(atttypid))
3383	{
3384		Oid			type_id = exprType(expr);
3385
3386		expr = coerce_to_target_type(pstate, expr, type_id,
3387									 atttypid, atttypmod,
3388									 COERCION_ASSIGNMENT,
3389									 COERCE_IMPLICIT_CAST,
3390									 -1);
3391		if (expr == NULL)
3392			ereport(ERROR,
3393					(errcode(ERRCODE_DATATYPE_MISMATCH),
3394					 errmsg("column \"%s\" is of type %s"
3395							" but default expression is of type %s",
3396							attname,
3397							format_type_be(atttypid),
3398							format_type_be(type_id)),
3399					 errhint("You will need to rewrite or cast the expression.")));
3400	}
3401
3402	/*
3403	 * Finally, take care of collations in the finished expression.
3404	 */
3405	assign_expr_collations(pstate, expr);
3406
3407	return expr;
3408}
3409
3410/*
3411 * Take a raw CHECK constraint expression and convert it to a cooked format
3412 * ready for storage.
3413 *
3414 * Parse state must be set up to recognize any vars that might appear
3415 * in the expression.
3416 */
3417static Node *
3418cookConstraint(ParseState *pstate,
3419			   Node *raw_constraint,
3420			   char *relname)
3421{
3422	Node	   *expr;
3423
3424	/*
3425	 * Transform raw parsetree to executable expression.
3426	 */
3427	expr = transformExpr(pstate, raw_constraint, EXPR_KIND_CHECK_CONSTRAINT);
3428
3429	/*
3430	 * Make sure it yields a boolean result.
3431	 */
3432	expr = coerce_to_boolean(pstate, expr, "CHECK");
3433
3434	/*
3435	 * Take care of collations.
3436	 */
3437	assign_expr_collations(pstate, expr);
3438
3439	/*
3440	 * Make sure no outside relations are referred to (this is probably dead
3441	 * code now that add_missing_from is history).
3442	 */
3443	if (list_length(pstate->p_rtable) != 1)
3444		ereport(ERROR,
3445				(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_COLUMN_REFERENCE),
3446				 errmsg("only table \"%s\" can be referenced in check constraint",
3447						relname)));
3448
3449	return expr;
3450}
3451
3452/*
3453 * CopyStatistics --- copy entries in pg_statistic from one rel to another
3454 */
3455void
3456CopyStatistics(Oid fromrelid, Oid torelid)
3457{
3458	HeapTuple	tup;
3459	SysScanDesc scan;
3460	ScanKeyData key[1];
3461	Relation	statrel;
3462	CatalogIndexState indstate = NULL;
3463
3464	statrel = table_open(StatisticRelationId, RowExclusiveLock);
3465
3466	/* Now search for stat records */
3467	ScanKeyInit(&key[0],
3468				Anum_pg_statistic_starelid,
3469				BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_OIDEQ,
3470				ObjectIdGetDatum(fromrelid));
3471
3472	scan = systable_beginscan(statrel, StatisticRelidAttnumInhIndexId,
3473							  true, NULL, 1, key);
3474
3475	while (HeapTupleIsValid((tup = systable_getnext(scan))))
3476	{
3477		Form_pg_statistic statform;
3478
3479		/* make a modifiable copy */
3480		tup = heap_copytuple(tup);
3481		statform = (Form_pg_statistic) GETSTRUCT(tup);
3482
3483		/* update the copy of the tuple and insert it */
3484		statform->starelid = torelid;
3485
3486		/* fetch index information when we know we need it */
3487		if (indstate == NULL)
3488			indstate = CatalogOpenIndexes(statrel);
3489
3490		CatalogTupleInsertWithInfo(statrel, tup, indstate);
3491
3492		heap_freetuple(tup);
3493	}
3494
3495	systable_endscan(scan);
3496
3497	if (indstate != NULL)
3498		CatalogCloseIndexes(indstate);
3499	table_close(statrel, RowExclusiveLock);
3500}
3501
3502/*
3503 * RemoveStatistics --- remove entries in pg_statistic for a rel or column
3504 *
3505 * If attnum is zero, remove all entries for rel; else remove only the one(s)
3506 * for that column.
3507 */
3508void
3509RemoveStatistics(Oid relid, AttrNumber attnum)
3510{
3511	Relation	pgstatistic;
3512	SysScanDesc scan;
3513	ScanKeyData key[2];
3514	int			nkeys;
3515	HeapTuple	tuple;
3516
3517	pgstatistic = table_open(StatisticRelationId, RowExclusiveLock);
3518
3519	ScanKeyInit(&key[0],
3520				Anum_pg_statistic_starelid,
3521				BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_OIDEQ,
3522				ObjectIdGetDatum(relid));
3523
3524	if (attnum == 0)
3525		nkeys = 1;
3526	else
3527	{
3528		ScanKeyInit(&key[1],
3529					Anum_pg_statistic_staattnum,
3530					BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_INT2EQ,
3531					Int16GetDatum(attnum));
3532		nkeys = 2;
3533	}
3534
3535	scan = systable_beginscan(pgstatistic, StatisticRelidAttnumInhIndexId, true,
3536							  NULL, nkeys, key);
3537
3538	/* we must loop even when attnum != 0, in case of inherited stats */
3539	while (HeapTupleIsValid(tuple = systable_getnext(scan)))
3540		CatalogTupleDelete(pgstatistic, &tuple->t_self);
3541
3542	systable_endscan(scan);
3543
3544	table_close(pgstatistic, RowExclusiveLock);
3545}
3546
3547
3548/*
3549 * RelationTruncateIndexes - truncate all indexes associated
3550 * with the heap relation to zero tuples.
3551 *
3552 * The routine will truncate and then reconstruct the indexes on
3553 * the specified relation.  Caller must hold exclusive lock on rel.
3554 */
3555static void
3556RelationTruncateIndexes(Relation heapRelation)
3557{
3558	ListCell   *indlist;
3559
3560	/* Ask the relcache to produce a list of the indexes of the rel */
3561	foreach(indlist, RelationGetIndexList(heapRelation))
3562	{
3563		Oid			indexId = lfirst_oid(indlist);
3564		Relation	currentIndex;
3565		IndexInfo  *indexInfo;
3566
3567		/* Open the index relation; use exclusive lock, just to be sure */
3568		currentIndex = index_open(indexId, AccessExclusiveLock);
3569
3570		/*
3571		 * Fetch info needed for index_build.  Since we know there are no
3572		 * tuples that actually need indexing, we can use a dummy IndexInfo.
3573		 * This is slightly cheaper to build, but the real point is to avoid
3574		 * possibly running user-defined code in index expressions or
3575		 * predicates.  We might be getting invoked during ON COMMIT
3576		 * processing, and we don't want to run any such code then.
3577		 */
3578		indexInfo = BuildDummyIndexInfo(currentIndex);
3579
3580		/*
3581		 * Now truncate the actual file (and discard buffers).
3582		 */
3583		RelationTruncate(currentIndex, 0);
3584
3585		/* Initialize the index and rebuild */
3586		/* Note: we do not need to re-establish pkey setting */
3587		index_build(heapRelation, currentIndex, indexInfo, true, false);
3588
3589		/* We're done with this index */
3590		index_close(currentIndex, NoLock);
3591	}
3592}
3593
3594/*
3595 *	 heap_truncate
3596 *
3597 *	 This routine deletes all data within all the specified relations.
3598 *
3599 * This is not transaction-safe!  There is another, transaction-safe
3600 * implementation in commands/tablecmds.c.  We now use this only for
3601 * ON COMMIT truncation of temporary tables, where it doesn't matter.
3602 */
3603void
3604heap_truncate(List *relids)
3605{
3606	List	   *relations = NIL;
3607	ListCell   *cell;
3608
3609	/* Open relations for processing, and grab exclusive access on each */
3610	foreach(cell, relids)
3611	{
3612		Oid			rid = lfirst_oid(cell);
3613		Relation	rel;
3614
3615		rel = table_open(rid, AccessExclusiveLock);
3616		relations = lappend(relations, rel);
3617	}
3618
3619	/* Don't allow truncate on tables that are referenced by foreign keys */
3620	heap_truncate_check_FKs(relations, true);
3621
3622	/* OK to do it */
3623	foreach(cell, relations)
3624	{
3625		Relation	rel = lfirst(cell);
3626
3627		/* Truncate the relation */
3628		heap_truncate_one_rel(rel);
3629
3630		/* Close the relation, but keep exclusive lock on it until commit */
3631		table_close(rel, NoLock);
3632	}
3633}
3634
3635/*
3636 *	 heap_truncate_one_rel
3637 *
3638 *	 This routine deletes all data within the specified relation.
3639 *
3640 * This is not transaction-safe, because the truncation is done immediately
3641 * and cannot be rolled back later.  Caller is responsible for having
3642 * checked permissions etc, and must have obtained AccessExclusiveLock.
3643 */
3644void
3645heap_truncate_one_rel(Relation rel)
3646{
3647	Oid			toastrelid;
3648
3649	/*
3650	 * Truncate the relation.  Partitioned tables have no storage, so there is
3651	 * nothing to do for them here.
3652	 */
3653	if (rel->rd_rel->relkind == RELKIND_PARTITIONED_TABLE)
3654		return;
3655
3656	/* Truncate the underlying relation */
3657	table_relation_nontransactional_truncate(rel);
3658
3659	/* If the relation has indexes, truncate the indexes too */
3660	RelationTruncateIndexes(rel);
3661
3662	/* If there is a toast table, truncate that too */
3663	toastrelid = rel->rd_rel->reltoastrelid;
3664	if (OidIsValid(toastrelid))
3665	{
3666		Relation	toastrel = table_open(toastrelid, AccessExclusiveLock);
3667
3668		table_relation_nontransactional_truncate(toastrel);
3669		RelationTruncateIndexes(toastrel);
3670		/* keep the lock... */
3671		table_close(toastrel, NoLock);
3672	}
3673}
3674
3675/*
3676 * heap_truncate_check_FKs
3677 *		Check for foreign keys referencing a list of relations that
3678 *		are to be truncated, and raise error if there are any
3679 *
3680 * We disallow such FKs (except self-referential ones) since the whole point
3681 * of TRUNCATE is to not scan the individual rows to be thrown away.
3682 *
3683 * This is split out so it can be shared by both implementations of truncate.
3684 * Caller should already hold a suitable lock on the relations.
3685 *
3686 * tempTables is only used to select an appropriate error message.
3687 */
3688void
3689heap_truncate_check_FKs(List *relations, bool tempTables)
3690{
3691	List	   *oids = NIL;
3692	List	   *dependents;
3693	ListCell   *cell;
3694
3695	/*
3696	 * Build a list of OIDs of the interesting relations.
3697	 *
3698	 * If a relation has no triggers, then it can neither have FKs nor be
3699	 * referenced by a FK from another table, so we can ignore it.  For
3700	 * partitioned tables, FKs have no triggers, so we must include them
3701	 * anyway.
3702	 */
3703	foreach(cell, relations)
3704	{
3705		Relation	rel = lfirst(cell);
3706
3707		if (rel->rd_rel->relhastriggers ||
3708			rel->rd_rel->relkind == RELKIND_PARTITIONED_TABLE)
3709			oids = lappend_oid(oids, RelationGetRelid(rel));
3710	}
3711
3712	/*
3713	 * Fast path: if no relation has triggers, none has FKs either.
3714	 */
3715	if (oids == NIL)
3716		return;
3717
3718	/*
3719	 * Otherwise, must scan pg_constraint.  We make one pass with all the
3720	 * relations considered; if this finds nothing, then all is well.
3721	 */
3722	dependents = heap_truncate_find_FKs(oids);
3723	if (dependents == NIL)
3724		return;
3725
3726	/*
3727	 * Otherwise we repeat the scan once per relation to identify a particular
3728	 * pair of relations to complain about.  This is pretty slow, but
3729	 * performance shouldn't matter much in a failure path.  The reason for
3730	 * doing things this way is to ensure that the message produced is not
3731	 * dependent on chance row locations within pg_constraint.
3732	 */
3733	foreach(cell, oids)
3734	{
3735		Oid			relid = lfirst_oid(cell);
3736		ListCell   *cell2;
3737
3738		dependents = heap_truncate_find_FKs(list_make1_oid(relid));
3739
3740		foreach(cell2, dependents)
3741		{
3742			Oid			relid2 = lfirst_oid(cell2);
3743
3744			if (!list_member_oid(oids, relid2))
3745			{
3746				char	   *relname = get_rel_name(relid);
3747				char	   *relname2 = get_rel_name(relid2);
3748
3749				if (tempTables)
3750					ereport(ERROR,
3751							(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
3752							 errmsg("unsupported ON COMMIT and foreign key combination"),
3753							 errdetail("Table \"%s\" references \"%s\", but they do not have the same ON COMMIT setting.",
3754									   relname2, relname)));
3755				else
3756					ereport(ERROR,
3757							(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
3758							 errmsg("cannot truncate a table referenced in a foreign key constraint"),
3759							 errdetail("Table \"%s\" references \"%s\".",
3760									   relname2, relname),
3761							 errhint("Truncate table \"%s\" at the same time, "
3762									 "or use TRUNCATE ... CASCADE.",
3763									 relname2)));
3764			}
3765		}
3766	}
3767}
3768
3769/*
3770 * heap_truncate_find_FKs
3771 *		Find relations having foreign keys referencing any of the given rels
3772 *
3773 * Input and result are both lists of relation OIDs.  The result contains
3774 * no duplicates, does *not* include any rels that were already in the input
3775 * list, and is sorted in OID order.  (The last property is enforced mainly
3776 * to guarantee consistent behavior in the regression tests; we don't want
3777 * behavior to change depending on chance locations of rows in pg_constraint.)
3778 *
3779 * Note: caller should already have appropriate lock on all rels mentioned
3780 * in relationIds.  Since adding or dropping an FK requires exclusive lock
3781 * on both rels, this ensures that the answer will be stable.
3782 */
3783List *
3784heap_truncate_find_FKs(List *relationIds)
3785{
3786	List	   *result = NIL;
3787	List	   *oids;
3788	List	   *parent_cons;
3789	ListCell   *cell;
3790	ScanKeyData key;
3791	Relation	fkeyRel;
3792	SysScanDesc fkeyScan;
3793	HeapTuple	tuple;
3794	bool		restart;
3795
3796	oids = list_copy(relationIds);
3797
3798	/*
3799	 * Must scan pg_constraint.  Right now, it is a seqscan because there is
3800	 * no available index on confrelid.
3801	 */
3802	fkeyRel = table_open(ConstraintRelationId, AccessShareLock);
3803
3804restart:
3805	restart = false;
3806	parent_cons = NIL;
3807
3808	fkeyScan = systable_beginscan(fkeyRel, InvalidOid, false,
3809								  NULL, 0, NULL);
3810
3811	while (HeapTupleIsValid(tuple = systable_getnext(fkeyScan)))
3812	{
3813		Form_pg_constraint con = (Form_pg_constraint) GETSTRUCT(tuple);
3814
3815		/* Not a foreign key */
3816		if (con->contype != CONSTRAINT_FOREIGN)
3817			continue;
3818
3819		/* Not referencing one of our list of tables */
3820		if (!list_member_oid(oids, con->confrelid))
3821			continue;
3822
3823		/*
3824		 * If this constraint has a parent constraint which we have not seen
3825		 * yet, keep track of it for the second loop, below.  Tracking parent
3826		 * constraints allows us to climb up to the top-level constraint and
3827		 * look for all possible relations referencing the partitioned table.
3828		 */
3829		if (OidIsValid(con->conparentid) &&
3830			!list_member_oid(parent_cons, con->conparentid))
3831			parent_cons = lappend_oid(parent_cons, con->conparentid);
3832
3833		/*
3834		 * Add referencer to result, unless present in input list.  (Don't
3835		 * worry about dupes: we'll fix that below).
3836		 */
3837		if (!list_member_oid(relationIds, con->conrelid))
3838			result = lappend_oid(result, con->conrelid);
3839	}
3840
3841	systable_endscan(fkeyScan);
3842
3843	/*
3844	 * Process each parent constraint we found to add the list of referenced
3845	 * relations by them to the oids list.  If we do add any new such
3846	 * relations, redo the first loop above.  Also, if we see that the parent
3847	 * constraint in turn has a parent, add that so that we process all
3848	 * relations in a single additional pass.
3849	 */
3850	foreach(cell, parent_cons)
3851	{
3852		Oid			parent = lfirst_oid(cell);
3853
3854		ScanKeyInit(&key,
3855					Anum_pg_constraint_oid,
3856					BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_OIDEQ,
3857					ObjectIdGetDatum(parent));
3858
3859		fkeyScan = systable_beginscan(fkeyRel, ConstraintOidIndexId,
3860									  true, NULL, 1, &key);
3861
3862		tuple = systable_getnext(fkeyScan);
3863		if (HeapTupleIsValid(tuple))
3864		{
3865			Form_pg_constraint con = (Form_pg_constraint) GETSTRUCT(tuple);
3866
3867			/*
3868			 * pg_constraint rows always appear for partitioned hierarchies
3869			 * this way: on the each side of the constraint, one row appears
3870			 * for each partition that points to the top-most table on the
3871			 * other side.
3872			 *
3873			 * Because of this arrangement, we can correctly catch all
3874			 * relevant relations by adding to 'parent_cons' all rows with
3875			 * valid conparentid, and to the 'oids' list all rows with a zero
3876			 * conparentid.  If any oids are added to 'oids', redo the first
3877			 * loop above by setting 'restart'.
3878			 */
3879			if (OidIsValid(con->conparentid))
3880				parent_cons = list_append_unique_oid(parent_cons,
3881													 con->conparentid);
3882			else if (!list_member_oid(oids, con->confrelid))
3883			{
3884				oids = lappend_oid(oids, con->confrelid);
3885				restart = true;
3886			}
3887		}
3888
3889		systable_endscan(fkeyScan);
3890	}
3891
3892	list_free(parent_cons);
3893	if (restart)
3894		goto restart;
3895
3896	table_close(fkeyRel, AccessShareLock);
3897	list_free(oids);
3898
3899	/* Now sort and de-duplicate the result list */
3900	list_sort(result, list_oid_cmp);
3901	list_deduplicate_oid(result);
3902
3903	return result;
3904}
3905
3906/*
3907 * StorePartitionKey
3908 *		Store information about the partition key rel into the catalog
3909 */
3910void
3911StorePartitionKey(Relation rel,
3912				  char strategy,
3913				  int16 partnatts,
3914				  AttrNumber *partattrs,
3915				  List *partexprs,
3916				  Oid *partopclass,
3917				  Oid *partcollation)
3918{
3919	int			i;
3920	int2vector *partattrs_vec;
3921	oidvector  *partopclass_vec;
3922	oidvector  *partcollation_vec;
3923	Datum		partexprDatum;
3924	Relation	pg_partitioned_table;
3925	HeapTuple	tuple;
3926	Datum		values[Natts_pg_partitioned_table];
3927	bool		nulls[Natts_pg_partitioned_table] = {0};
3928	ObjectAddress myself;
3929	ObjectAddress referenced;
3930	ObjectAddresses *addrs;
3931
3932	Assert(rel->rd_rel->relkind == RELKIND_PARTITIONED_TABLE);
3933
3934	/* Copy the partition attribute numbers, opclass OIDs into arrays */
3935	partattrs_vec = buildint2vector(partattrs, partnatts);
3936	partopclass_vec = buildoidvector(partopclass, partnatts);
3937	partcollation_vec = buildoidvector(partcollation, partnatts);
3938
3939	/* Convert the expressions (if any) to a text datum */
3940	if (partexprs)
3941	{
3942		char	   *exprString;
3943
3944		exprString = nodeToString(partexprs);
3945		partexprDatum = CStringGetTextDatum(exprString);
3946		pfree(exprString);
3947	}
3948	else
3949		partexprDatum = (Datum) 0;
3950
3951	pg_partitioned_table = table_open(PartitionedRelationId, RowExclusiveLock);
3952
3953	/* Only this can ever be NULL */
3954	if (!partexprDatum)
3955		nulls[Anum_pg_partitioned_table_partexprs - 1] = true;
3956
3957	values[Anum_pg_partitioned_table_partrelid - 1] = ObjectIdGetDatum(RelationGetRelid(rel));
3958	values[Anum_pg_partitioned_table_partstrat - 1] = CharGetDatum(strategy);
3959	values[Anum_pg_partitioned_table_partnatts - 1] = Int16GetDatum(partnatts);
3960	values[Anum_pg_partitioned_table_partdefid - 1] = ObjectIdGetDatum(InvalidOid);
3961	values[Anum_pg_partitioned_table_partattrs - 1] = PointerGetDatum(partattrs_vec);
3962	values[Anum_pg_partitioned_table_partclass - 1] = PointerGetDatum(partopclass_vec);
3963	values[Anum_pg_partitioned_table_partcollation - 1] = PointerGetDatum(partcollation_vec);
3964	values[Anum_pg_partitioned_table_partexprs - 1] = partexprDatum;
3965
3966	tuple = heap_form_tuple(RelationGetDescr(pg_partitioned_table), values, nulls);
3967
3968	CatalogTupleInsert(pg_partitioned_table, tuple);
3969	table_close(pg_partitioned_table, RowExclusiveLock);
3970
3971	/* Mark this relation as dependent on a few things as follows */
3972	addrs = new_object_addresses();
3973	ObjectAddressSet(myself, RelationRelationId, RelationGetRelid(rel));
3974
3975	/* Operator class and collation per key column */
3976	for (i = 0; i < partnatts; i++)
3977	{
3978		ObjectAddressSet(referenced, OperatorClassRelationId, partopclass[i]);
3979		add_exact_object_address(&referenced, addrs);
3980
3981		/* The default collation is pinned, so don't bother recording it */
3982		if (OidIsValid(partcollation[i]) &&
3983			partcollation[i] != DEFAULT_COLLATION_OID)
3984		{
3985			ObjectAddressSet(referenced, CollationRelationId, partcollation[i]);
3986			add_exact_object_address(&referenced, addrs);
3987		}
3988	}
3989
3990	record_object_address_dependencies(&myself, addrs, DEPENDENCY_NORMAL);
3991	free_object_addresses(addrs);
3992
3993	/*
3994	 * The partitioning columns are made internally dependent on the table,
3995	 * because we cannot drop any of them without dropping the whole table.
3996	 * (ATExecDropColumn independently enforces that, but it's not bulletproof
3997	 * so we need the dependencies too.)
3998	 */
3999	for (i = 0; i < partnatts; i++)
4000	{
4001		if (partattrs[i] == 0)
4002			continue;			/* ignore expressions here */
4003
4004		ObjectAddressSubSet(referenced, RelationRelationId,
4005							RelationGetRelid(rel), partattrs[i]);
4006		recordDependencyOn(&referenced, &myself, DEPENDENCY_INTERNAL);
4007	}
4008
4009	/*
4010	 * Also consider anything mentioned in partition expressions.  External
4011	 * references (e.g. functions) get NORMAL dependencies.  Table columns
4012	 * mentioned in the expressions are handled the same as plain partitioning
4013	 * columns, i.e. they become internally dependent on the whole table.
4014	 */
4015	if (partexprs)
4016		recordDependencyOnSingleRelExpr(&myself,
4017										(Node *) partexprs,
4018										RelationGetRelid(rel),
4019										DEPENDENCY_NORMAL,
4020										DEPENDENCY_INTERNAL,
4021										true /* reverse the self-deps */ );
4022
4023	/*
4024	 * We must invalidate the relcache so that the next
4025	 * CommandCounterIncrement() will cause the same to be rebuilt using the
4026	 * information in just created catalog entry.
4027	 */
4028	CacheInvalidateRelcache(rel);
4029}
4030
4031/*
4032 *	RemovePartitionKeyByRelId
4033 *		Remove pg_partitioned_table entry for a relation
4034 */
4035void
4036RemovePartitionKeyByRelId(Oid relid)
4037{
4038	Relation	rel;
4039	HeapTuple	tuple;
4040
4041	rel = table_open(PartitionedRelationId, RowExclusiveLock);
4042
4043	tuple = SearchSysCache1(PARTRELID, ObjectIdGetDatum(relid));
4044	if (!HeapTupleIsValid(tuple))
4045		elog(ERROR, "cache lookup failed for partition key of relation %u",
4046			 relid);
4047
4048	CatalogTupleDelete(rel, &tuple->t_self);
4049
4050	ReleaseSysCache(tuple);
4051	table_close(rel, RowExclusiveLock);
4052}
4053
4054/*
4055 * StorePartitionBound
4056 *		Update pg_class tuple of rel to store the partition bound and set
4057 *		relispartition to true
4058 *
4059 * If this is the default partition, also update the default partition OID in
4060 * pg_partitioned_table.
4061 *
4062 * Also, invalidate the parent's relcache, so that the next rebuild will load
4063 * the new partition's info into its partition descriptor.  If there is a
4064 * default partition, we must invalidate its relcache entry as well.
4065 */
4066void
4067StorePartitionBound(Relation rel, Relation parent, PartitionBoundSpec *bound)
4068{
4069	Relation	classRel;
4070	HeapTuple	tuple,
4071				newtuple;
4072	Datum		new_val[Natts_pg_class];
4073	bool		new_null[Natts_pg_class],
4074				new_repl[Natts_pg_class];
4075	Oid			defaultPartOid;
4076
4077	/* Update pg_class tuple */
4078	classRel = table_open(RelationRelationId, RowExclusiveLock);
4079	tuple = SearchSysCacheCopy1(RELOID,
4080								ObjectIdGetDatum(RelationGetRelid(rel)));
4081	if (!HeapTupleIsValid(tuple))
4082		elog(ERROR, "cache lookup failed for relation %u",
4083			 RelationGetRelid(rel));
4084
4085#ifdef USE_ASSERT_CHECKING
4086	{
4087		Form_pg_class classForm;
4088		bool		isnull;
4089
4090		classForm = (Form_pg_class) GETSTRUCT(tuple);
4091		Assert(!classForm->relispartition);
4092		(void) SysCacheGetAttr(RELOID, tuple, Anum_pg_class_relpartbound,
4093							   &isnull);
4094		Assert(isnull);
4095	}
4096#endif
4097
4098	/* Fill in relpartbound value */
4099	memset(new_val, 0, sizeof(new_val));
4100	memset(new_null, false, sizeof(new_null));
4101	memset(new_repl, false, sizeof(new_repl));
4102	new_val[Anum_pg_class_relpartbound - 1] = CStringGetTextDatum(nodeToString(bound));
4103	new_null[Anum_pg_class_relpartbound - 1] = false;
4104	new_repl[Anum_pg_class_relpartbound - 1] = true;
4105	newtuple = heap_modify_tuple(tuple, RelationGetDescr(classRel),
4106								 new_val, new_null, new_repl);
4107	/* Also set the flag */
4108	((Form_pg_class) GETSTRUCT(newtuple))->relispartition = true;
4109
4110	/*
4111	 * We already checked for no inheritance children, but reset
4112	 * relhassubclass in case it was left over.
4113	 */
4114	if (rel->rd_rel->relkind == RELKIND_RELATION && rel->rd_rel->relhassubclass)
4115		((Form_pg_class) GETSTRUCT(newtuple))->relhassubclass = false;
4116
4117	CatalogTupleUpdate(classRel, &newtuple->t_self, newtuple);
4118	heap_freetuple(newtuple);
4119	table_close(classRel, RowExclusiveLock);
4120
4121	/*
4122	 * If we're storing bounds for the default partition, update
4123	 * pg_partitioned_table too.
4124	 */
4125	if (bound->is_default)
4126		update_default_partition_oid(RelationGetRelid(parent),
4127									 RelationGetRelid(rel));
4128
4129	/* Make these updates visible */
4130	CommandCounterIncrement();
4131
4132	/*
4133	 * The partition constraint for the default partition depends on the
4134	 * partition bounds of every other partition, so we must invalidate the
4135	 * relcache entry for that partition every time a partition is added or
4136	 * removed.
4137	 */
4138	defaultPartOid =
4139		get_default_oid_from_partdesc(RelationGetPartitionDesc(parent, true));
4140	if (OidIsValid(defaultPartOid))
4141		CacheInvalidateRelcacheByRelid(defaultPartOid);
4142
4143	CacheInvalidateRelcache(parent);
4144}
